strategy.name: van de Poppe + Tone Vays BTC Strategy
sources: Michaël van de Poppe (@CryptoMichNL) — EMA 21/50/200 pullback system
Tone Vays (@ToneVays) — MACD divergence + RSI confirmation

indicators.ema_21: Short-term trend. Price bouncing off this = healthy uptrend.
indicators.ema_50: Medium-term trend. EMA 21 above EMA 50 = bullish alignment.
indicators.ema_200: Macro trend filter. Only take longs above this. Only take shorts below.
indicators.rsi_14: Momentum filter. Pullback entry zone: 35–58. Overbought: >70. Oversold: <30.
indicators.macd_12_26_9: Tone Vays momentum confirmation. Line above signal = bullish. Divergence = reversal warning.

bullish: MACD line is above signal line
bearish: MACD line is below signal line
neutral: Price is chopping around the EMA 200
EMA 21 and EMA 50 are flat or intertwined

long: Macro bias is BULLISH (price > EMA 200, EMA 21 > EMA 50)
Price pulls back into the EMA 21–50 zone
RSI cools to 35–58 range (not capitulation, just a healthy pullback)
MACD line remains above signal line (momentum intact)
Enter in two tranches: 50% on first touch of zone, 50% on confirmed bounce candle
short: Macro bias is BEARISH (price < EMA 200, EMA 21 < EMA 50)
Price retraces up into the EMA 21–50 zone

exit_rules: Take 33–50% profit at prior swing high or key resistance
Remaining position targets: 1.272 and 1.618 Fibonacci extensions from prior swing
Full exit if stop loss is hit — no averaging down into losers
Exit if MACD crosses bearish on the entry timeframe
risk_rules: Risk maximum 1–2% of portfolio per trade
Minimum R:R of 2:1 — prefer 3:1 or better
Stop loss placed below the 0.786 Fibonacci level or most recent swing low
Position size = risk amount divided by distance to stop loss
If stop is hit, setup is invalid — do not re-enter immediately
No trading against the EMA 200 macro trend
notes: Add macro context here — e.g. upcoming Fed meetings, halving cycle position, key on-chain levels, Bitcoin dominance trend, anything that should affect bias this week.
